World stocks hold near record highs, oil keeps below $100 on peace deal hopesSINGAPORE/LONDON, April 17 (Reuters) - World stocks held near record highs on Friday and were set for their third straight week of gains, while benchmark oil prices were pinned below $100 a barrel ahead of a crucial weekend that could pave the way for a near-term resolution ‌of the Iran war. President Donald Trump expressed confidence that an agreement could soon be reached to end the conflict and urged the Tehran-aligned Hezbollah group ‌to hold its fire as a 10-day truce went into effect betw

Crude oil slumps 10% and Wall Street rallies toward another record after Strait of Hormuz opensOil prices are falling by more than 10%, and Wall Street is rallying toward another record after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is fully open, which would allow oil tankers to exit the Persian Gulf again and carry crude to customers worldwide.  The S&P 500 rallied 0.7% as U.S. stocks sprinted toward the finish of a third straight week of big gains.  Stocks have rallied more than 11% since late March on hopes that the United States and Iran can avoid a worst-case scenario for the global economy.
A Lebanon Ceasefire and Potential Iran Peace Talks Push Oil Prices DownOil prices fell as a fragile Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and renewed hopes for U.S.-Iran talks eased immediate supply fears.

Oil falls on prospects for talks to end Iran war and revive supplyBy Helen Clark PERTH, April 17 (Reuters) - Oil prices fell in early trade on Friday on optimism the Middle East conflict could be nearing an end after a 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel

Fed's Miran says inflation picture has deteriorated — but still favors multiple rate cuts this yearFederal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran suggested on Thursday that he's become slightly more hawkish because inflation looks a bit stickier and he now sees less reason for accommodative monetary policy than he once did.

An AI economic boom may be underway, and economists say the US is best positioned to benefitUS GDP could grow by more than 10% by 2034 on the back of large-scale economic growth driven by the boom in AI, according to BNP Paribas economists.

Fed's Williams warns Iran war could boost inflation, hurt growthNew York Federal Reserve president John Williams said Thursday that the Middle East conflict has introduced "substantial risks" and heightened uncertainty for the US economy, signaling the central bank is intent on holding interest rates steady.
Some companies are pulling back on hiring thanks to AIAI is leading some businesses to pump the brakes on hiring plans, according to the Federal Reserve's Beige Book.

Fed's Beige Book: Iran war has businesses on edgeThe war in Iran is complicating investment and hiring plans and creating new cost pressures for businesses, according to the Federal Reserve's Beige Book released Wednesday.
Tax Day: Refunds are up as filing deadline hitsApril 15 — today — is the last day for filers to get their tax return in and pay their bill without incurring penalties.
